originalTitle: Leading anti‑racism activist in Tunisia jailed for eight years
title: A Brave Helper: Standing Up for Kindness in Tunisia!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter from sunny Africa, ready to share an important story with you. Today, we are going to talk about a very special lady named Saadia. She lives in a beautiful country in North Africa called Tunisia. Saadia is a helper, a real superhero of kindness! She believes everyone should be treated fairly, no matter where they come from or what they look like. Let's learn about her brave heart.

Who is Saadia? A Friend to Everyone!

Saadia Mosbah is a kind woman. She loves to help people. She is what we call an 'activist.' An activist is someone who works very hard to make the world a better place for everyone. Saadia believes that all people are important. She thinks everyone deserves kindness and respect. She teaches people that it is not right to be mean to someone just because they are different. That meanness is called 'racism.' Saadia fights against racism. She wants everyone to feel safe and happy in their homes, even if their homes are new. She helps people who are new to a place. Did you know? Tunisia is a country in North Africa. It has lovely beaches and ancient cities. It's a place where many different people live and share their lives. Saadia wants to make sure everyone feels welcome there.

What Are Migrants? Our New Friends!

So, who are these 'new friends' that Saadia helps? We call them 'migrants.' Migrants are people who move from one country to another. Imagine if your family moved to a brand new town, far away from your old school and friends. That's a bit like being a migrant, but often, it's a much longer journey across borders! People become migrants for many reasons. Sometimes, they move to find a safer place to live because their home country might have problems. Sometimes, they look for food or jobs so they can take care of their families. They hope to find a happy new life somewhere else. They pack their bags and travel, hoping for a kind welcome. Saadia wants to make sure they get that kind welcome. Did you know? Many animals migrate too! Birds fly to warmer places in winter, and some fish swim far to lay their eggs. People also move to find what they need, just like the animals.

What Happened in Tunisia?

One day, the President of Tunisia, his name is Mr. Kais Saied, said something that made people worry. He said that too many of these new friends, the migrants, were coming to Tunisia. He said they were a 'demographic threat.' That's a big, tricky word, isn't it? It means he thought there were too many new people. He worried that these new people would change the country too much. This idea made some people in Tunisia feel less kind towards migrants. It made some people think it was okay to be mean to them. But Saadia knew in her heart that this was not right. She knew that everyone, new or old, deserved kindness. She knew that even if there are many people, everyone can still be friends and share. Did you know? The world has over 8 billion people! Even with so many people, we can all learn to live together happily if we are kind and share.

Saadia Stands Up for Kindness!

When Saadia heard these words, she didn't get scared. Instead, she got even braver! She decided she had to speak up and help more. She reminded everyone that all people deserve love and respect. She said, 'It doesn't matter if someone has a different skin color or speaks a different language. We are all humans, and we all deserve to be treated with a warm heart!' She campaigned for migrants. That means she worked hard to tell people that these new friends needed help and understanding, not unkindness. She organized groups and talked to people. She told everyone that racism is a very bad thing. Racism is when you are unkind to someone just because they are different from you. Saadia believes this is wrong. She showed everyone how to be a good friend to these new neighbours. Did you know? The Golden Rule is a very old and wise saying: 'Treat others the way you want to be treated.' Saadia lives by this rule every single day!

The Sad News: What Happened to Saadia?

Now, here's the part that makes many people sad. Because Saadia was so brave and spoke up for the migrants, she was put in jail. Jail is a place where people go when they break the rules, or when other people think they did something wrong. It is very sad that someone who was just trying to help others and spread kindness is now in jail. Many people around the world think this is not fair. They believe Saadia was doing a good thing. They think she should be free to continue her important work. It reminds us that sometimes, being brave and standing up for what is right can be hard. But Saadia showed us that it is always worth it to be kind. Did you know? Nelson Mandela, a very famous leader from South Africa, also spent many years in jail because he fought for fairness and equality. His bravery changed the world!

Why Is This Important for Us?

This story of Saadia is very important for all of us, even you, my young friends. It teaches us many valuable lessons. First, it reminds us to **always be kind to everyone**. Imagine how you would feel if you were new somewhere and no one was friendly to you. We should always try to make new friends feel welcome. Second, it teaches us to **stand up for what is right**. If you see someone being mean to another person because they are different, you can be brave like Saadia and say, 'That's not fair!' You can tell a grown-up or show kindness yourself. Third, it teaches us that **racism is wrong**. Everyone is special and unique, and we should celebrate our differences, not use them to be unkind. Did you know? Every single person in the world is unique, just like a snowflake! We all have special talents and things that make us wonderful. Imagine how boring the world would be if we were all exactly the same! So, let's remember Saadia Mosbah, the brave woman who helps others. Let's remember her message of kindness and fairness. We can all be little Saadias in our own lives, spreading smiles and helping hands. Together, we can make Africa and the whole world a kinder, happier place for everyone. Thank you for listening, and remember to always choose kindness!
summary: Saadia Mosbah, a kind helper in Tunisia, was put in jail for eight years because she bravely spoke up and helped people who moved to her country, after the President said unkind things about them.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us to always be kind to everyone, no matter where they come from, and to be brave like Saadia by standing up for what is right and speaking out against meanness or racism.

originalTitle: Air raid sirens blare during Eid call to prayer in Kuwait
title: Loud Noises During a Special Day in Kuwait!
content:
Hello, my amazing young friends across Africa! It’s your friendly news reporter here, ready to share a story from a place far, far away. Imagine a land with lots of sunshine and beautiful buildings. This land is called Kuwait. It's a country, just like Ghana or Kenya or South Africa, but it's on a different continent. Sometimes, big news happens in faraway places, and it's good to learn about it, even if it sounds a little bit grown-up. We'll make it super simple and easy to understand, I promise! Today, we’re talking about some very loud sounds that happened in Kuwait during a very special time for many people.

A Special Day and a Loud Sound

In Kuwait, many people were celebrating a super happy holiday called Eid. Eid is a time for families to get together, share yummy food, give gifts, and pray. It's like a big party that lasts for a few days! People put on their best clothes and visit friends and relatives. It’s a joyful time for millions of people all over the world, including some of your neighbours and friends here in Africa. But on this special day, something very unexpected happened. Suddenly, very loud noises started to sound all across the big city in Kuwait. These noises were not happy party sounds. They were called air raid sirens. Imagine a really, really loud horn that goes 'Wooooo-oooooh!' over and over again, like a giant, very noisy whistle that never stops! It was a sound that told people to be careful and stay safe. It must have been a bit scary for everyone to hear such a loud and strange sound during their happy celebration. **Did you know?** Eid happens twice a year, and each time it’s a reason for lots of happiness and sharing! Millions of people celebrate Eid all around the world, from Africa to Asia and beyond!

What Are Air Raid Sirens?

So, what exactly are these air raid sirens? Well, they are very important alarm bells, but super-sized! They are like the loud bells you hear at school for break time, but much, much louder and with a different sound. Sirens are there to warn people about possible danger. If there's something that could be unsafe, like a big storm coming or if something is wrong, these sirens make a big noise to tell everyone to pay attention and find a safe place. It's like when your parents tell you to be careful if you are near a busy road. The siren is a very loud voice telling everyone, 'Be safe! Take cover!' It's a way for big towns and cities to talk to everyone at once. When these sirens sound, people usually know they should go inside, stay away from windows, or go to a special safe spot, just in case. They are like a big, loud protector, making sure everyone knows when to be extra careful. They might sound scary, but they are actually there to help keep everyone safe and sound. **Did you know?** You might have heard other kinds of sirens before! Fire trucks, police cars, and ambulances all have their own sirens that make loud noises to let everyone know they are on their way to help someone!

Why Did the Sirens Blare?

Now, you might be wondering, 'Why did these loud sirens sound in Kuwait on such a happy day?' Well, this part is a little bit grown-up, but we can understand it simply. There are two other countries, Iran and the United States, that were not feeling very friendly towards each other at that time. Sometimes, countries, just like people, can have arguments and disagreements. In this case, one country, Iran, was upset with the United States. So, Iran sent some things called 'missiles' and 'drones' towards places where the United States had some of its important things and helpers. Imagine a missile like a super-fast rocket that flies through the air! And drones are like flying robots – some are small like a toy, and some can be quite big! They fly without a pilot inside. They were aimed at places where US helpers, who are like soldiers, work or live. It's like if two friends were arguing, and one threw a toy near the other's house to show they were upset. When these things were flying around, the people in Kuwait heard about it. Even though Kuwait was not part of the argument, they wanted to make sure all their people were safe, just in case. That’s why they turned on the big, loud air raid sirens. It was a precaution, like when your teacher tells you to be quiet in the library, even if you weren't the one making noise, just to make sure everyone follows the rules. **Did you know?** Drones are not just for serious things! Many people use small drones to take amazing pictures and videos from high up in the sky, or even to deliver small packages!

Kuwait and Staying Safe

The most important thing to remember is that everyone in Kuwait was safe. The sirens did their job. They warned people to be careful. The governments of Kuwait and other countries worked hard to make sure nothing bad happened to the innocent people there. It was a moment of worry, but because people listened to the warnings and stayed calm, everything turned out okay in the end. Kuwait is a beautiful country, and its people are friendly and strong. They want peace, just like we all do here in Africa. When countries have disagreements, it’s always best if they can talk to each other and find a way to be friends again, instead of having big arguments. Just like you might have a disagreement with a friend, but then you talk about it and say sorry and play together again. That's what grown-ups hope countries will do too. This story reminds us that even when things sound scary, there are always people working to keep us safe. And talking things out is always better than fighting. Everyone in Kuwait, during their special Eid holiday, got through this moment of loud sirens, and now they are looking forward to peaceful days filled with joy and happiness, just like they always do. **Did you know?** Kuwait is known for having some of the world's largest oil reserves! That's why it's a very important country in that part of the world!

What This Means for Us

So, what can we learn from this story, my clever young friends? Even though Kuwait is very far away, we can understand some important lessons. One big lesson is about safety. Always listen to warnings from your parents, teachers, or community leaders. They tell you things to keep you safe and sound. Another lesson is about being kind and trying to solve problems with words, not with anger or loud noises. When countries, or even just people, have disagreements, it's always, always better to talk about it peacefully. It’s like when you have a problem with your brother or sister. Instead of yelling or getting upset, you can sit down and talk about how you feel. Peace is a wonderful thing. Peace means a time of quiet and calm, with no trouble, war, or violence. It's something everyone hopes for! We can all try to bring more peace to our own homes, our schools, and our communities here in Africa. We can be good neighbours and kind friends. And when we hear about big news from far away, we can remember that kindness and talking are always the best ways to make the world a better, safer, and happier place for everyone.
summary: During a happy holiday called Eid, loud air raid sirens sounded in Kuwait because two other countries, Iran and the United States, were having a big disagreement, but everyone in Kuwait stayed safe.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows us how important it is for countries to talk and be friends, not fight, and it reminds us to always listen to warnings to stay safe, just like we do at home or school.

content: Crown Princess Mette Marit tells Norwegian TV she wishes she had never met the late sex offender.

originalTitle: Israel strikes Syria after Druze clashes
title: News from Far Away: Countries, Friends, and Being Fair!
content:
Hello, amazing friends all over Africa! It's your friendly news reporter here, ready to tell you about some important things happening far, far away. Sometimes, like on a playground, countries can have big disagreements. But guess what? We always hope they can talk and be friends again, just like you and your pals!

Meet Our Neighbours: Syria and Israel

Imagine you live in a big neighbourhood. All the houses are countries! Far from us, there are two countries next to each other called Syria and Israel. They are like neighbours, sharing a border, which is like an invisible line on the ground that shows where one country ends and another begins. Did you know? Having neighbours is wonderful because you can share smiles and sometimes even food! But sometimes, neighbours can have big worries. Syria is a land with ancient cities and lovely sunshine. Israel is also a land with beautiful places and lots of history. Both countries have many, many people who call them home.

A Special Group of People: The Druze

In these lands, there are many different groups of people. One special group is called the Druze. They are like a big family with their own special traditions and ways of doing things. Imagine your school has different classes, or your village has different families. The Druze are one of these special groups who live in both Syria and Israel. They have homes and families in these places. Did you know? The world is full of different cultures and people, and that's what makes it so exciting and colourful! It's good to learn about everyone. Sometimes, things can get tricky when different groups live close to each other, especially when they are across those invisible borders.

What Happened? A Sad Disagreement

Recently, there was a big worry. Some people, called the Druze, had some big problems, almost like a serious argument. When people have these big arguments, we call them 'clashes'. It means they were not agreeing and things were difficult. Israel felt very worried about its people, especially those Druze people who live close to the border. They felt they needed to protect them, like a big brother or sister looks out for you. So, Israel sent some special flying machines, like big, fast aeroplanes, to fly over parts of Syria. These aeroplanes did something we call 'strikes'. This means they aimed at certain places in Syria. Did you know? Talking is usually the best way to solve problems. Even when things are hard, talking can help make things better. These strikes were not meant to hurt people, but to hit special places. It's like when you have a big problem, sometimes people try to send a very strong message.

Syria Was Upset

When Israel did this, Syria became very, very upset. Imagine someone coming into your house without asking, or touching your toys. You wouldn't like that, would you? Syria said that Israel had no right to do this. They felt that their 'house' – their country – should be safe and that only Syria should make decisions about what happens inside its borders. This is called 'sovereignty'. Did you know? 'Sovereignty' is a big word, but it just means that every country gets to be the boss of itself! No other country should tell it what to do or hit its places without a very good reason and permission. Syria said that these strikes were a very 'outrageous' thing to do. That means they thought it was terribly wrong and unfair. They were very, very angry and sad that their country was hit.

What Are 'Weapons Sites' and 'Buildings'?

When the news says Israel hit 'weapons sites' and 'government infrastructure', what does that mean? 'Weapons sites' are places where countries keep special tools, like important equipment, that they use to protect themselves. It's like a special shed where a farmer keeps his tools. They are not toys, but serious things. 'Government infrastructure' just means important buildings that the country uses to help its people. Like big offices where leaders work, or places that help deliver water or electricity to homes. These buildings help run the country, just like your school building helps you learn. Did you know? Governments are like the grown-ups in charge of a country. They try to make sure everyone is safe and has what they need. So, Israel hit these kinds of places because they felt it was important to send a message related to the worries they had about the Druze people and safety.

Why This Matters to Everyone

Even though Syria and Israel are far away, what happens there can remind us of important lessons. It reminds us that all countries, just like all people, want to be safe and live in peace. It also reminds us that when there are big disagreements, it is always, always best to talk. Like when you have a problem with a friend, you talk to them instead of getting angry or hitting. Did you know? Many countries in Africa work together with their neighbours to share resources and help each other. It's a great example of being friendly neighbours! Everyone, especially children like you, deserves to grow up in a world where everyone is safe and happy. We all hope that the leaders of Syria and Israel can find a way to talk and solve their problems peacefully.

Our Wish for Peace

So, even when we hear news about big disagreements, let's remember to always hope for peace. Let's hope that all countries can learn to be good neighbours, helping each other and sharing the sunshine. Thank you for listening, my wonderful friends!
summary: Far away in the Middle East, two countries, Syria and Israel, are having a disagreement, with Israel hitting places in Syria after worries about a special group of people called the Druze, which made Syria very upset about its country being touched.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows how countries sometimes have big problems, but we always hope they can talk and be friends, so everyone can live in peace and safety, just like we want for our own homes and friends.

content: The band's 10th album sees them return to the rebellious, rap-heavy sound of their early days.

originalTitle: One final experiment? Unpicking Tuchel's latest England squad
title: Super Squad News for England!
content:
Hello, Super Football Fans!

Hello, my wonderful friends from all over Africa! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you exciting news from the world of football. Today, we're talking about the England football team! They are getting ready for some very big games. Imagine your favourite team preparing for the biggest match ever – that's what's happening now. We love football because it brings everyone together. It makes us cheer, laugh, and sometimes even jump for joy! Today's news is all about the special group of players who might play for England soon. It’s a very important time for them.

What is a Football Squad?

First, let's talk about what a 'squad' is. You know how you have a team of friends for a game? Well, a football team also has a group of players. This group is called a squad. It's usually more players than can play at one time. Why? So the coach can pick the very best players for each match. Imagine you have a box of your favourite crayons. You pick the red one for a fiery sun, and the blue one for the cool sky. A coach picks players like you pick crayons – the best one for the job! A squad makes sure there are always enough good players ready to play. It's like having many tools in a toolbox, ready for any job. Did you know? A football team usually has 11 players on the pitch at one time! But a squad can have 20, 25, or even more players so they have lots of choices.

Meet the Coach, Mr. Thomas Tuchel!

Today's big news is about the coach, Mr. Thomas Tuchel. He is the person who leads the England team. He helps them practice, tells them what to do, and chooses who gets to play. It's a very big job! He has to be very smart and know a lot about football. Mr. Tuchel has just picked a special, bigger group of players for the England squad. This means he has chosen more players than usual. It’s like when you have a party, and you invite extra friends just in case! He wants to make sure he has all the best players ready for the big games ahead. He spends a lot of time watching players. Did you know? A coach's job is not just to pick players, but also to teach them new tricks and help them play better together as a team!

More Players, More Fun!

This bigger squad is very exciting! It means more players get a chance to show what they can do. Imagine being a player who hasn't played for England in a long time. You've been working hard, practicing every day, hoping for another chance. And now, Mr. Tuchel has given you that chance! This is like when you try really hard at school, and your teacher gives you a special star. It makes you feel happy and proud! For some players, getting into this big squad is a dream come true. They will train super hard to show the coach they are ready for the World Cup. Did you know? Some football players start playing when they are very little, just like you might play with a ball in your garden! They practice for many, many years to become good enough to play for their country.

Giving Players Another Chance

Some players might be called 'forgotten men'. This doesn't mean anyone forgot them! It just means they haven't played for the England team in a while. Maybe they were hurt, or maybe other players were chosen instead. But now, Mr. Tuchel is giving them hope! He wants to see if they can help the team win. It’s like when you have a toy you haven't played with for a long time, and then you remember how much fun it is! These players are very happy to be back. They want to prove they are strong, fast, and ready to score goals. This is a very good thing for the team because it means more strong players are available. Did you know? Even the best players in the world can get hurt. It takes a lot of hard work and patience to get better and play football again!

What Are the Big Games? (The World Cup!)

The England team is getting ready for something called the World Cup. Wow, what is that? The World Cup is the biggest football party in the whole wide world! Teams from many, many countries play against each other to see who is the very best. It happens only once every four years. It’s like the biggest sports day you can imagine, but for the whole planet! Everyone watches, everyone cheers. Mr. Tuchel choosing this big squad is to help England get ready for this huge event. He wants to make sure England has the strongest team possible so they can try to win the World Cup! Did you know? The World Cup trophy is made of gold! The winning team gets to lift it high above their heads. Many children dream of doing that one day.

Getting Ready is Important

But even with all these great players, there can be some small challenges. The news says there might be 'problems'. This doesn't mean bad things! It just means the coach, Mr. Tuchel, has a lot of thinking to do. He needs to make sure all the players play well together. Sometimes, new players need time to learn how to play with the old players. It’s like when you and your friends play a new game. You need to practice together so everyone knows what to do. The coach needs to find the best way for all the players to work as one big, happy, winning team. This practice time is very important before the World Cup starts.

Let's Cheer Them On!

So, this special, bigger squad is a chance for England to get ready, give more players a chance, and make sure they are super strong for the World Cup. It's a very exciting time for football fans everywhere, including us here in Africa! Let’s all cheer for the England team and Mr. Thomas Tuchel as they prepare. We hope they play their very best and have lots of fun. Maybe one day, you too will be a famous football player! Keep practicing and dreaming big! We'll be watching and cheering them on! Did you know? Football is the most popular sport in the world! Billions of people watch it and play it. Maybe you play it too!
summary: Coach Thomas Tuchel has picked a bigger England football squad, giving more players a chance to play and helping the team get ready for the big World Cup games, even though there are still things to work on.
whyMatters: This news is important because it means the England football team is getting ready for the biggest football party, the World Cup, by choosing many strong players, which gives more people a chance to play for their country and makes the team better prepared to win.

title: No contact, no World Cup place? What went wrong for Alexander-Arnold?
content: Trent Alexander-Arnold's omission from Thomas Tuchel's biggest England squad is the latest blow to his stop-start international career and casts huge doubt on his hopes of playing at the World Cup.
